policy.rule: Action surfacing and early-retirement impact outrank UI polish.

items.title: Current holdings tab with combined, SKB, parents, and sleeve views
items.why: Useful for understanding concentration and account ownership, but lower priority than making ACT_NOW decisions durable.
items.done_when: Cockpit can switch combined/SKB/parents views and show sleeve concentration without confusing ETF look-through with direct holdings.

items.title: Broker PDF intake and trade-diff reconciliation
items.why: The system needs current account positions after uploaded broker PDFs, including trade diffs since the last snapshot.
items.done_when: Uploaded account PDFs produce account-level positions, combined positions, and a clear diff of buys/sells/trims since the prior snapshot.

items.title: ETF look-through and overlapping sleeve exposure
items.why: Sleeve concentration can be misleading when ETFs overlap with single-name AI, nuclear, or crypto exposure.
items.done_when: Dashboard distinguishes direct sleeve weights from effective look-through exposure and labels estimates clearly.

items.title: Harden Gmail-based Fundstrat intake
items.why: Fundstrat is the source spine; full-body emails should feed source calls and top prospects without storing raw publication bodies.
items.done_when: Routine reliably searches, reads full bodies when permitted, redacts bodies, updates source calls/top prospects, and reports snippet-only discovery as not checked.

items.title: Handle Tom Lee and Fundstrat video updates through transcripts or captions
items.why: Some Tom Lee macro updates are video-first. They may matter, but video extraction is lower priority than text FlashInsights, email full-body intake, and dashboard action reliability.
items.done_when: Authenticated Fundstrat video pages can produce compact transcript-derived rows only when a visible transcript, captions, companion article text, or user-supplied compact notes exist; video-only titles and thumbnails remain discovery/audit-only.

items.title: Merge Fundstrat source-call candidates during full-body intake
items.why: Source-call calibration should not depend on a forgotten second command after full-body Fundstrat intake.
items.done_when: The canonical Fundstrat intake command can update source_calls.json, log_call_dates.json, and source_call_cache_summary.json from classified full-body candidates while snippet-only discovery leaves those caches untouched.

items.title: Parse direct Fundstrat monthly uploads into compact Bible deck state
items.why: Monthly Fundstrat updates arrive as uploaded PDFs and should update the source spine without storing noisy stock-price chart text.
items.done_when: Direct monthly PDF/text/JSON uploads can write fundstrat_bible.json, preserve useful summary sections, route Top-5/Bottom-5 and separate consider-list rows to top_prospects.json, and avoid raw PDF text, Core List tables, or stock-price chart clutter.

items.title: Replace Claude cloud routines with Codex automations
items.why: Recurring acquisition, synthesis, and research queue work should run from repo-owned Codex routine definitions.
items.done_when: All daily/off-hours routines have active Codex automations, repo-owned prompts, and clear not-checked behavior.

items.title: Map which state belongs in Notion, GitHub, or both
items.why: Overlapping storage caused analysis to exist without surfacing. Ownership rules reduce drift.
items.done_when: Each major object type has a source-of-truth owner, mirror policy, and dashboard surfacing path.

items.title: Relabel From-Research priority so it is not confused with conviction
items.why: The From-Research lane maps queue priority into the shared action row confidence field, which can blur research urgency with investment conviction.
items.done_when: The canonical dashboard labels From-Research priority distinctly while keeping action rows and Today's Actions conviction labels clear.

items.title: Use structured ticker fields for Research Queue action promotion
items.why: Off-hours dossiers should promote actionable buy/sell/hold/research items from explicit ticker metadata instead of fragile title parsing.
items.done_when: Research action promotion prefers ticker/symbol fields, preserves process-item filtering, and activates the near-term date clause for plain-title ticker dossiers.

items.title: Validate the Collection-to-Analyst handoff before feed assembly
items.why: Bad source stamps or inconsistent collection metadata can silently contaminate action surfacing before the publish gate ever sees a feed.
items.done_when: Full-build and runtime skeleton paths fail closed on malformed Contract-B snapshots, bad run/source stamps, critical missing sources, and staleness/source-failure drift.

items.title: Accept live connector-shaped Catalyst Calendar rows
items.why: Near-term catalysts should become durable ACT_NOW review items without requiring a manual calendar export shape.
items.done_when: Catalyst intake accepts stdin connector envelopes, flattens Notion-style properties, writes catalysts.json, and preserves full-build ownership of action promotion.

items.title: Extract a shared dashboard action card renderer
items.why: Today's Actions and From Research should not drift apart when action clarity, priority labels, aging, sizing, or goal-impact badges change.
items.done_when: Both lanes render through one ActionCard component while preserving lane-specific footer text, From Research priority labeling, and Today's Actions aging/sizing chips.

items.title: Surface Signal Log rows as watch-only cockpit context
items.why: Morning Scan notes can explain attention and timing, but should not silently create buy/sell actions without a sharper source.
items.done_when: Optional signal_log convention files feed a separately classified dashboard lane, validate row shape, report not-checked when absent, and never promote directly into Today's Actions.

items.title: Keep generated HTML from implying all-clear when lanes are dark
items.why: The static GitHub Pages summary can be seen before the canonical cockpit, so empty actions plus not-checked lanes must not look like a clean day.
items.done_when: Generated HTML labels itself as a summary/export view, surfaces lane-status and feedback context, and shows a caveat when Today's Actions is empty with dark or stale lanes.

items.title: Distinguish cross-source from same-source conflict wording
items.why: Mixed conviction can come from independent source disagreement or from two analysts inside one source group; the action read should not overstate independence.
items.done_when: The analyst read preserves conflict scope/detail, cross-source cases still say cross-source split, and same-source Lee/Farrell disagreement renders as same-source split without changing Mixed/flat logic.

items.title: Support richer structured Daily Synthesis action fields
items.why: Daily Synthesis can become more actionable when explicit rows carry timing, sizing, capital effect, and evidence without requiring free-form prose guessing.
items.done_when: Structured synthesis actions accept explicit ticker aliases, recommendation text, urgency, time window, capital effect, sizing, goal channels, and missing evidence while vague prose remains unpromoted.

items.title: Surface supplied daily/weekly event-risk scans
items.why: Fast exogenous shocks such as oil, war, rates, or volatility events can move the book before normal source lanes update.
items.done_when: Supplied event-risk rows write event_risks.json, report missing scans as not checked, and promote high/critical events into conservative exposure-review actions.

items.title: Evaluate queued system upgrades after live feed is re-verified
items.why: Queued Notion/Claude-build ideas should be promoted only when they improve conviction, actionability, risk control, sizing, timing, or usability.
items.done_when: System Update Queue and CI Update Queue candidates are reviewed against the Investing OS goal, with only high-signal slices promoted and low-value complexity left deferred.
items.outcome: Promoted event-risk intake, offensive catalyst review framing, compact monthly Fundstrat state, and dark-lane next-step guidance. Left Core List table ingestion, stock-specific AVGO work, and options/IV automation out of tonight's basic build.

items.title: Frame high-conviction catalysts as offensive review prompts
items.why: Near-term catalysts on high-conviction names should force a ready offensive plan, not only a defensive risk check.
items.done_when: T1/T2 non-MONITOR held catalysts ask for add-to-target, defined-risk upside, and dip-ladder review while MONITOR catalysts remain watch-only and all actual sizing still goes through the gate.

items.title: Populate compact monthly Fundstrat state from direct PDF upload
items.why: The live cockpit should use the available monthly Fundstrat source without storing noisy Core List tables or chart text.
items.done_when: The May 2026 monthly PDF writes compact fundstrat_bible and top_prospects convention files, dark lanes shrink, and uncorroborated quiet Bottom 5 rows stay out of Today's Actions.

items.title: Separate scheduled fired proof from fresh boundary-data proof
items.why: Scheduled proof can be green even when a routine fires but writes no fresh artifact. Operators need fired, committed, no-op, and stale-boundary outcomes split apart.
items.done_when: Routine receipts and ops views distinguish fired from produced fresh committed boundary data for each cockpit-critical artifact.

items.title: Require explicit operator interpretation for UW supports or contradicts
items.why: A successful UW fetch is not a direction. The system needs a named mechanism for turning inconclusive endpoint rows into supports or contradicts without auto-promoting neutral.
items.done_when: Neutral UW rows remain inconclusive unless a committed operator interpretation tied to the captured row explicitly marks confirmed or contradicted.
items.outcome: Added uw_endpoint_interpret.py plus full-build wiring. Operator overlays must target a captured neutral row by mode, endpoint, ticker, and checked_at; non-empty dirty overlays fail closed before the cockpit feed can derive from them.

items.title: Keep the local dashboard visibly tied to the freshest scheduled build
items.why: A stale local tab can contradict origin/main scheduled proof and make operators distrust the cockpit.
items.done_when: The local server either tracks the latest scheduled origin/main artifact or clearly reports checkout, branch, commit, feed timestamp, and hash in the UI and health endpoint.

items.title: Keep source scoring honest until graded outcomes reach threshold
items.why: Source scoring should refresh daily but remain off until enough closed graded calls exist. Lowering thresholds or fake backfills would create false confidence.
items.done_when: source_rates regenerates from real source_calls, reports n=0 or insufficient data honestly, and flips on only when n>=15 per scored source/tier.

items.title: Make Fundstrat inbox checked/not-checked state explainable
items.why: The cockpit must say whether the Fundstrat inbox was actually checked this render, not hide Gmail/Chrome gaps behind cached daily calls.
items.done_when: Fundstrat intake surfaces last checked time, access path, snippet-only/full-body counts, and explicit not_checked reasons in the feed.

items.title: Make the cockpit show why nothing is actionable
items.why: When there are no ACT_NOW rows, the operator needs the blocking reason, next proof needed, sizing implication, and time sensitivity rather than a quiet dashboard.
items.done_when: The primary cockpit surfaces blocked-but-important candidates with proof gaps, timing sensitivity, sizing impact, and next operator step.

items.title: Defer conviction tuning until honest proof rails are stable
items.why: Weight and threshold tuning can make the dashboard look more actionable before the proof and data-freshness contracts are reliable.
items.done_when: After boundary proof, interpretation, local freshness, source scoring, and decision-surface gaps are stable, revisit conviction weights without weakening honesty rails.
